enrichment.summary: Kyle shares his morning routine, discusses a creative project called 'Boulder Rome' where he maps ancient Roman territories onto Boulder County geography, and mentions listening to Gibbon's Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire with his girlfriend each evening.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

stances.position: Most fascinating thing he has ever done; finds the geographic match between Boulder County and the Latin Kingdom magical
Accepts viewer feedback that five to eight minutes is the sweet spot; aims for exactly five minutes

entities: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire (Gibbon)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle just got out of bed and is having breakfast while being really high before going to work.

claims.subject: Kyle's videos
claims.claim: Kyle received a request that his videos should be at least five minutes long but no longer than eight minutes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to follow the guideline of making his videos five to eight minutes long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday was Kyle's first day dedicating the whole day to working on Boulder Rome, specifically the kingdom.

claims.subject: Boulder Rome
claims.claim: The kingdom of Boulder Rome is exactly the size of Boulder County.

claims.subject: Boulder County
claims.claim: Boulder County traces back to 200.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: The Latins trace Rome back to 206.

claims.subject: Caribou
claims.claim: Caribou is going to be all the longer, and Kyle is changing some stuff around, including locating the Cluelian trench.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to create Boulder Rome organically by pairing cities without following any system and piecing it together later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle traced the outlines of cities to determine habitable areas, aiming for around 4k, similar to his work in Europe and Italy.

claims.subject: Manzanilla
claims.claim: Manzanilla is a perfect oval, but Kyle thinks he is missing some details and needs to find some walls he made a lot of time ago.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today is Kyle's first day spending the entire day working on Boulder Rome.

claims.subject: Boulder Rome
claims.claim: Kyle finds Boulder Rome super fascinating and magical due to how well the cities and sizes match up with Boulder County and the kingdom of the Latins.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to work on Boulder Rome for the next two or three weeks before focusing on Boulder itself.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is almost done with Boulder but needs to work on panoramas before completing it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle listened to the chapter about Gratian from 'Decline and Fall' yesterday.

claims.subject: Gratian
claims.claim: Kyle wants to memorize the part about the conversion and the split of the Roman Empire into two, as well as the conversion of religions.

claims.subject: Roman emperors
claims.claim: Kyle finds some of the more obscure Roman emperors interesting and mentions Adrian Murdoch, who does good synopses of the emperors.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has a system with his girlfriend where they talk for an hour or two each night about the book they listen to together.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes that shooting for exactly five minutes of video content each day is enough to cover everything effectively.

transcript.text: Hello, it's 8.02 a.m. I just got out of bed and having some breaky and got really high and going to go to work. I got a request from yesterday that my videos should be at least five minutes long, but no longer than eight. So five to eight is the sweet spot. So thank you. Thank you for the inside information.

Um, I'll try to follow the guidelines. Um, so yesterday went to work, came home. Yeah, but I think I said that yesterday. Anyways, came home. Um, and then, uh, yesterday was my first day working on, um, where I dedicated the whole day of working, working on, um,

Boulder Rome, the city and the empire. Oh, well, actually I was doing the kingdom. Uh, kingdom is exactly, uh, the size of Boulder County. So that's pretty cool. Boulder County traced 200 and Boulder Rome or bolt. I mean, Rome, the Latins trace at 206. That's pretty cool. That's your conference.

So, um, um, Caribou is going to be all the longer. I'm changing some stuff around, you know, I located the Cluelian trench and it's all this kind of stuff. So that's what I'm going to do. Um, I'm going to create it out organically. I'm going to pair cities, but I'm going to do it organically, not by any system. And I'll, I'll piece it together later. Cause I don't even really know, I guess.
So, but it's pretty cool because when I traced the outlines of the, uh, cities that I would think that they would be like the habitable area or whatever.
It was kind of around 4k, which is what I, um, you know, was shooting for when I was doing it all through Europe. I mean, Italy and stuff, because that's what Manzanilla is.
You should check out Manzanilla. It's like a perfect oval, but I think I'm missing some stuff. I need to find some stuff, some walls that I made a lot of time.
So, um, so today will be my first, um, one day where, cause I don't work today. So I'll work tomorrow. But today's my first, uh, day where I spend the entire day on Boulder Rome.
So I'm going to just see how far I can go, how much I can do today. It's super, super, super, super, super fascinating. So far, this is the most fascinating thing I've ever done.
And so far it's totally magical how, um, how, uh, they match up so well, the cities and the sizes, the county and the kingdom of the Latins. Um, so, um, yeah.

Yeah. And then I'll try to put it on the site for download, kind of like the same way I have that other stuff. Um, and so, yeah, I'll just go full steam ahead on those for like the next two or three weeks.
Until I get to a point where I really want to do Boulder itself. Cause I can't do Boulder. I can't do the city Boulder Rome and create it completely until I'm done with Boulder.
And I'm not even done with Boulder, but I'm almost done. But before I can get done with Boulder, I was working on the panoramas.
So I'll have to go and I'll work on this until I, you know, do that. And I'll go on the panoramas of Boulder and then do Boulder.

Yeah. And I probably won't even be done with that by the end of the year, but, um, so, uh, let's see. I listened to, um, I was doing, uh, Decline the Fall and I was doing, uh, Gratian, the chapter about Gratian yesterday.
Yeah. I want to memorize the part about, uh, the, the conversion and the, the split of the empire into two and the conversion of the religions and stuff. That's interesting stuff.

And some of the more obscure emperors, there's this guy, Adrian Murdoch, who does good synopsis of the emperors, but to just memorize all of it, I have a cool system going on with my girlfriend where we talk like an hour or two a night about the book.

We listen to the book together, like an hour a day. So we like kind of learn together. So that's pretty cool. Um, so, okay. So yeah, going five minutes is actually good. Just shoot for exactly five minutes a day. Um, and you can get, if you do five minutes, I think that's enough to get everything. Boom. Tied up perfectly. So I'll talk to y'all tomorrow. Peace out.
